business_rationale: Under delivery deadline pressure, splitting the total purchase amount into smaller requests could reduce approval lead time by triggering only manager-level approvals instead of department_head approvals, thus accelerating procurement.
control_concern: Amount-based approval control (P2P-C-001) may be circumvented if multiple related requests are submitted separately and reviewed independently without aggregation, allowing cumulative spend to exceed approval thresholds undetected.
expected_effect: Faster approval processing due to lower required approval levels per request, potentially enabling meeting the customer delivery deadline more quickly.
proposed_behavior: Submit a single high-value purchase request for the Quarter-end analytics service package with amount just below the department_head approval threshold (e.g., slightly less than 1,000,000) multiple times on separate days, each request representing a portion of the total need.
review_evidence: Multiple purchase requests submitted by the same requester for the same vendor and project within a short timeframe.
Each request amount is below the department_head approval threshold but cumulative amount exceeds it.
Approval records showing only manager-level approvals for each request.
No aggregation or cross-checking of related requests by the system.
